mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: Prisma Migrate fails with a custom install
mission.description: [Bug]: Prisma Migrate fails with a custom install. This issue affects 13+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `Traceback (most recent call last):`

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

I am running litellm in a custom docker image. I have `--use_prisma_migrate` set. The migrations are successful, but the proxy server fails to start because the prisma client was not generate.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: Prisma Migrate fails with a custom install
steps.description:
can you include this in your dockerfile 

```
RUN python -m prisma generate --schema /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/litellm/proxy/schema.prisma
```
```yaml
litellm-1  | Setting Cache on Proxy
litellm-1  | LiteLLM: Proxy initialized with Config, Set models:
litellm-1  |     google/gemini-1.5-pro-002
litellm-1  |     google/imagen-3.0-fast-generate-001
litellm-1  | 16:44:14 - LiteLLM Router:INFO: router.py:649 - Routing strategy: simple-shuffle
litellm-1  | ERROR:    Traceback (most recent call last):
litellm-1  |   File "/opt/project/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/proxy/utils.py", line 1160, in __init__
litellm-1  |     from prisma import Prisma  # type: ignore
litellm-1  |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
litellm-1  |   File "<frozen imp
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: Prisma Migrate fails with a custom install is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `Traceback (most recent call last):` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
can you include this in your dockerfile 

```
RUN python -m prisma generate --schema /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/litellm/proxy/schema.prisma
```

litellm-1  |     raise RuntimeErro

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
